entries.notes: Browser code reads shared constants through TEAM_BUILDER_PUBLIC_HELPERS. Validators protect the public wrapper mirror.

entries.area: final-round eligible teams and fixture authority
entries.notes: Active candidate leakage checks now use shared eligibility helpers and active QA assertions.
The browser active-stage filter delegates to shared projection checks.
Budget, captain, objective components, team counts, and fixture counts are summarized through shared helpers.
Pure validation is centralized. Browser adapter rows and live-state wiring remain local to script.js.

entries.area: country-limit and budget-limit derivation by matchday
entries.notes: Live browser helpers still derive matchday limits locally. This is a good next extraction because it is rules-derived and lower-risk than optimizer scoring.
Shared position validation exists, but browser-specific formation and requirement parsing still lives beside UI state.
Strategy player score, optimizer state ranking, captain recommendation score, and artifact strategic score should remain untouched until exact score fixtures are added.
Validation of captain/vice presence is centralized, but ranking/recommendation logic is still behavior-sensitive and should be deferred.
A generated or sync-checked wrapper would remove the clearest remaining non-scoring drift risk.
Small local formatter duplication in validators is acceptable because it does not affect public behavior.

recommended_next_extraction: Rules-derived budget, country-limit, and formation/squad-requirement helpers, or a generated/synced browser helper wrapper. Scoring and captain ranking should wait.
